summary: Independent Candidate A Wave 4 product/API review passed with no findings. V11 matches the owner goal, exposes a closed additive public API, remains IDE/model/provider neutral, and truthfully leaves agent-runtime effects to external hosts.

artifacts.name: product-api-review.md
artifacts.content:
# Product/API Review

Verdict: PASS

No product or public-API findings were identified.

## Owner Goal
V11 accepts a reviewed closed GoalContract, constructs and selects task-shaped specialist partitions with a visible serial baseline, emits exact AgentBlueprints, and renders and verifies a two-digest-bound package. Semantic decomposition, approval, context delivery, execution, isolation, persistence, integration, merge, and memory mutation remain external as required.

## Public API
The four public operations are separately named and exported: deriveTaskAuthorityProjection, compileAgentBlueprints, renderSpecialistPackage, and verifySpecialistPackage. The additive specialist types cover the closed input, authority projection, compilation, blueprint, manifest, rendered package, and trusted expectation. GoalContract and SpecialistCompilationRequest are closed at runtime, and the schema and TypeScript declarations agree on exactly five permission-demand kinds.

## Neutrality
No role, provider, model, prompt, executor, credential, grant, runtime profile, agent profile, or assignment field appears in the reviewed specialist input contract. The generated blueprint describes task demand rather than a persona. Provider-specific translation and runtime supply are explicitly external. The Node.js package requirement is an implementation runtime requirement, not selection of an agent runtime, model, provider, IDE, or executor.

## Product Truthfulness
README, normative contract, IDE kickoff, renderer, and two-phase review documentation consistently label V11 as an implementation candidate and do not claim that core launches agents, enforces authority, reserves capacity, integrates changes, or mutates memory. Projected launch waves are described only as deterministic planning values.

## Usability
The kickoff provides a short one-message path for bounded goals while preserving clarification gates, compact roster explanation, manifest-based contract resolution, two-digest approval, and a conditional Audit B flow when an agent must inspect the exact compilation that defines Candidate A.

## Stop Conditions
A generic role cannot substitute for this contract's exact product/API objective, authenticated source set, evidence duty, and stop boundary. The public specialist contract requires no provider or external agent runtime. Neither REVISE condition is triggered.

Audit B's semantic verdict was not used as a substitute for this independent review.

evidence.criterion: The V11 product and public API match the owner goal and remain IDE, model, and provider neutral.

assumptions: The supplied external-host check-evidence PASS is the trust boundary for domain-separated compilation, package, Audit B, and cross-package authorization verification; this specialist did not rerun that broader host process.
The authenticated persisted preparation handoff and the host-delivered inline handoff are the same complete object; its PASS was used only for candidate identity and freshness.
Node.js 22.14 or newer is the library implementation environment and does not constitute selection of an external agent runtime, IDE, model, provider, or executor.
The packed-consumer harness was reviewed as source evidence. This work unit did not execute npm packaging or consumer installation because its rendered authority was read-only.
risks: V11 cannot prove the semantic quality of human-authored atomic work units; the owner and IDE remain responsible for decomposition and acceptance design.
An external host can violate blueprint authority or ordering despite a valid package; runtime enforcement remains intentionally outside V11.

A separate prior-version declaration snapshot was not in this specialist's declared context. Compatibility review is therefore based on the additive root export inventory and installed-consumer fixture supplied by the contract.
Any mutation after the final 16-item freshness check invalidates this handoff.

failedAttempts.cause: PowerShell expanded the JSON Schema $defs token inside a double-quoted Node command, producing a JavaScript syntax error.
Nested shell quoting stripped JavaScript string delimiters, producing a JavaScript syntax error.
The PowerShell variable name $Host collided with the reserved host variable. Consumer-source assertions emitted non-terminating errors, so the apparent PASS output was rejected.
failedAttempts.effects: Earlier schema assertions passed, but the run was not accepted as evidence; no writes.

followUps: Preserve this complete raw handoff unchanged as product-api-review.md evidence.

Integrate only against Candidate A compilation sha256:3677db46ecd2a387239887ecff6d131f1d0616a2dba972ffbe63bdc0ee6b9984 and package sha256:1cc61cead6a953633c8369e29270c92318e9c58381e2389a971bad5e1ecc72b0.
Require later integration and release gates to reauthenticate their own declared context and preserve every raw specialist handoff.
No Candidate A revision is requested by this work unit.
